message:
feat(daemon): add spawn-guard file lock, run-lock exclusion, double-checked model warm, and reduce max request size

Daemon spawn race prevention:
- Add a file-based spawn-guard lock (daemon_spawn_guard_lock_path) so
  multiple concurrent client processes do not all attempt to auto-start
  the daemon simultaneously. The first client to acquire the exclusive
  lock spawns the daemon; subsequent clients re-check socket connectivity
  after acquiring the lock and skip the spawn if the daemon is already up.
- Add wait_for_spawned_daemon_ready() which polls the socket with
  try_wait on the child process, so the spawning client knows whether
  the daemon actually came up or exited early with a meaningful error.

Daemon run-lock exclusion:
- The daemon server itself now acquires an exclusive file lock
  (daemon_run_lock_path) at startup via try_lock_exclusive. If another
  instance already holds the lock, the daemon exits immediately with
  AddrInUse rather than silently fighting over the socket. This replaces
  the previous behavior where two daemon processes could race on the
  same socket path.
- The lock error now distinguishes WouldBlock (another instance running)
  from other IO errors, giving clearer diagnostics.

Model manager double-checked locking:
- warm_embedder() and warm_reranker() now use a fast-path read lock
  check followed by a slow-path write lock with a second check (double-
  checked locking pattern). Previously, two threads could both pass the
  read-lock check and redundantly load the model. Now the write lock is
  taken once and the inner check prevents duplicate work.

Protocol hardening:
- Reduce the maximum request frame size from 100 MB to 10 MB. Embedding
  requests are text-based and should never approach 10 MB; the previous
  100 MB limit could allow a misbehaving client to force excessive memory
  allocation.
- Replace dotenvy::var("USER") with std::env::var("USER") for the
  default socket path. dotenvy loads .env files which is unexpected for
  a system daemon path computation.

Worker cleanup:
- Replace the inline doc_id parsing logic in EmbeddingWorker with the
  shared parse_semantic_doc_id() utility function, reducing code
  duplication and ensuring consistent parsing across the codebase.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
